entries: {@i Choose A or B:} (A) {@item Light Crossbow|XPHB}, {@item Bolt|XPHB|20 Bolts}, {@item Scholar's Pack|XPHB}, {@item Hide Armor|XPHB}, {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB}, {@item Shortsword|XPHB}, {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB|Arcane Focus (Formula Book)}, and 15 GP; or (B) 75 GP

entries.name: Apothecary
Sidebar: Apothecary 2024
entries: We're considering the following updates to the Apothecary to bring the class in-line with the 2024 design philosophy:

items: First, like all classes in 2024 ruleset, an Apothecary chooses their subclass at 3rd level. All 1st-level subclass features are instead gained at 3rd level.

Second, Apothecaries learn one {@filter Esoteric Theory|optionalfeatures|source=!guidedrakkenheim|feature type=et} at 1st level. This increases their overall number of Esoteric Theories learned by one.
Apothecaries retain their increased number of Spell Slots compared to their Warlock cousins.
The Experimental Research feature is new at 2nd level.
{@homebrew Prepared spells amount changed to match Clerc/Druid 2024 progression.}
{@homebrew Added Epic Boon as a class feature similar to other classes in 2024.}
{@homebrew Translated the class to match 2024 styling without changing any of the mechanics outside of what was previously mentioned.}

entries: We may consider further game balance adjustments to individual subclasses, Esoteric Theories, or spells, but these changes represent the main changes we're looking at for the core class itself.

A bespectacled man stands at an operating table, studying the dissected monstrosity resting upon the slab. The crackle of electric conductors harmonize with his arcane mumblings as he takes up his scalpels and saws. As he finishes the final stitch, the creature's hand twitches and jolts to life once more.

Cloaked in an acid-stained heavy coat, a tall half-elf brews tinctures and tonics. Her face is concealed behind thick black goggles and a heavy breathing mask as she performs an unusual combination of herbalism, alchemy, medicine, and magic. Her mixtures bubble and boil as she muses whether the cure will be worse than the disease.

A lithe tiefling soothes a suffering patient as they draw a sample of contaminated blood. Lifting the vial to examine the liquid within, they pull upon eldritch energy to distill the sample into a pure contagion. Scribbling their findings down in a withered notebook, they add the vial to their collection of pathogens and scourges to use against their foes.

entries.name: Medical Experts
entries: An Apothecary is a practiced medical expert and arcane magic user. They wield their knowledge to both heal and harm, augmenting their conventional skills with alchemy and occult magic. Some apothecaries might first attempt treatment with traditional means like medicinal herbs and surgical techniques. When these mundane methods are not enough, they use arcane magic and eldritch forces to restore their patients. An Apothecary's unique skills prove useful whether their patients suffer from natural or supernatural ailments, but apothecaries possess knowledge of the body which can be used for more than just healing. Their arcane methods can strengthen, transform, or protect their allies, and some apothecaries know deadly techniques which can harm and kill their enemies. An Apothecary understands how the difference between a poison and a cure lies often only in the dose.

Their careful study of life and death gives an Apothecary the tools to both help allies survive horrible ailments, injuries, and pluck them from the clutches of death, or use their enemies as test subjects for their dangerous compounds, terrible diseases and poisonous magic.

entries.name: Knowledge Seekers
entries: Most apothecaries set out with the goal of furthering their studies and expanding their magical horizons, uncovering new ways to wield magic for dangerous or life-saving effects, and learning forbidden knowledge kept from the rest of the world. They believe that knowledge is power, and see themselves as seekers of knowledge. Some kinder apothecaries see their duty as protectors of the innocent, dedicating their lives to helping sick and injured people. Others see every opportunity to exploit the weak and push their search for true power forward. Many fall somewhere in between, riding the lines of mad scientist and renowned doctor.

entries.name: Occult Practitioners
entries: Apothecaries seek out occult lore and use it to study and practice scientific theories. Many of their magical incantations, whether it be for the purposes of saving lives, or ending them, are deemed experimental. Many apothecaries keep a notebook close at hand and write down esoteric theories based on their findings and field work.

Apothecaries are gaining renown within some remote communities on the continent as doctors and healers to whom one can turn to instead of religious figures. However, they are more often viewed as dangerous scientists and mysterious witches who use magic to create toxins and poisons that could eradicate entire armies.

entries.name: Creating an Apothecary
entries: When you set out to create an Apothecary, the most important question is to decide your path of study. What is it that drives you to seek knowledge? Are you working in the service of good? Is your life dedicated to helping those in need? By studying poisons, diseases, occult lore, and forbidden knowledge, do you hope to help the sick and wounded? Perhaps you aim to save someone you care about, or protect your kingdom or homeland from the evils of this world. Or maybe your studies are more dangerous in nature. Do you set out on adventure to test your dangerous chemical formulae? Do you see each monster or enemy as a test subject for your concoctions and spells?

All apothecaries share a search for knowledge, and dabble in forbidden lore and occult practices. Although many in their practice are happy to sell their services or potions in cities or to well-paying nobles and kings, some are called to adventure, setting out on the endless hunt for knowledge. Others work as investigators and researchers. Many apothecaries see adventure as a means to perform direct field research, perfect their skills, and test the true potential of their theories.

entries.name: Quick Build
entries: You can make an Apothecary quickly by following these suggestions.

items: First, Intelligence should be your highest ability score, followed by Constitution.
Second, choose your character ancestry and background.

Third, choose the {@spell Poison Needle|CommunityDrakkenheim} and {@spell Spare the Dying|XPHB} cantrips, along with the 1st-level spells {@spell Envenom|CommunityDrakkenheim} and {@spell Healing Word|XPHB}.

entries.name: Apothecaries in Drakkenheim
entries: Apothecaries can be found throughout the continent in many forms. The term is broadly used to describe many magic-adjacent professions found on the continent and the vast array of possible fields of study is ever expanding.

The Amethyst Academy has departments of apothecaries managing their labs and research centers. Within their many schools and strongholds, they teach advanced sciences and using magic to better understand the body and mind, and the various modern medical practices that can be combined with magic to save lives—or end them.

Not all apothecaries are mageborn. The broad term has been used to describe several practices that combine herbal ingredients, chemicals, and poisons with biological study to induce outcomes similar to the effects of evocation or necromantic magic. Pathogenists working in distant labs perform studies to create new powerful strains of disease and poison. Chemists find ways to surpass the potency of mageborn spellcasting with the right dose of chemicals for their explosive and destructive results. Other

Mutagenists working with the mutating effects of delerium seek to control and isolate the elements of mutation and apply them in a more controlled manner. There are even reports of apothecaries who are studying techniques to unlock the potential of the mind.
These Alienists have caused a stir amongst nobles, mages, and clergy for their ability to tap into mental capabilities that mirror magic, without requiring a mageborn's lineage.

Secretive reanimators pursue grisly studies into the realms of death and undeath, mimicking necromantic magic using little more than new science and invention. There are apothecaries working under the Sacred Flame as exorcists, channeling their occult knowledge and research into helping rid holy sites of undead, dispatch unwanted spirits, and remove terrible curses.

Apothecaries are found on every continent; whether mageborn or not, many are skilled academics who have a keen mind for pushing the boundaries of science. These researchers have found themselves on the path of the Apothecary, lending their aid to cities and towns throughout the continent, and occasionally bringing unique chemical warfare to the battlefield of great wars.

subclass.name: Reanimator
subclassFeatures: I Can't Stop The Monster I Created|Apothecary|EldritchDrakkenheim24|Reanimator|EldritchDrakkenheim24|18|EldritchDrakkenheim24

entries: Your arcane research has given you facility with spells. Refer to the Core Rules for the general rules of spellcasting and the Spells Listing in this book for the Apothecary spell list.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know three cantrips of your choice from the Apothecary spell list. You learn additional Apothecary cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Apothecary Features table. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can replace one of your cantrips from this feature with another Apothecary cantrip of your choice.
When you reach Apothecary levels 4 and 10, you learn another Apothecary cantrip of your choice, as shown in the Cantrips column of the Apothecary Features table.

entries.name: Formula Book
entries: You have a book or collection of notes that you study to prepare your Apothecary spells, develop your Esoteric Theories, and record your occult practices. You may use your formula book as an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} for your Apothecary spells. You may describe this book any way you like. Some examples of your book's appearance include:

items: A large leatherbound tome with yellowing pages bound shut with an iron lock
A scattered assortment of scribbled notes loosely held together by rope
A pristine journal with perfect edges and beautiful penmanship
A leather briefcase with two small golden locks
A medical bag with surgical symbols on the sides
A beat-up satchel slung over one shoulder with vials and bottles poking out of every pocket

entries: If the book is destroyed or lost, you can recreate it using inks and paper during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Apothecary Features table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your Apothecary spells of levels 1-5. The table also shows the level of those slots, all of which are the same level. To cast one of your Apothecary spells of 1st level or higher, you must expend a spell slot. You regain all expended Apothecary Magic spell slots when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

For example, when you're a level 5 Apothecary, you have three level 3 spell slots. To cast the level 1 spell {@spell Fog Cloud|XPHB}, you must spend one of those slots, and you cast it as a level 3 spell.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose four level 1 Apothecary spells.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Apothecary levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Apothecary Features table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional Apothecary spells until the number of spells on your list matches the number in the table. The chosen spells must be of a level no higher than what's shown in the table's Slot Level column for your level. When you reach level 6, for example, you learn a new Apothecary spell, which can be of levels 1-3.

If another Apothecary feature gives you spells that you always have prepared, those spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare with this feature, but those spells otherwise count as Apothecary spells for you.

entries.name: Changing Your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your list of prepared spells, replacing any of the spells there with other Apothecary spells for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Apothecary spells, since you learn your spells through dedicated study and occult knowledge. You use your Intelligence whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Intelligence modifier when setting the saving throw DC for an Apothecary spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} or your formula book as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Apothecary spells.

classFeature.name: Esoteric Theories
entries: You have discovered esoteric theories, scraps of forbidden lore that you can use to augment your magical abilities.

You gain one {@filter Esoteric Theory|optionalfeatures|source=!guidedrakkenheim|feature type=et} of your choice. Your theory options are detailed at the end of the class description. When you gain certain Apothecary levels, you gain additional theories of your choice, as shown in the Theories column of the Apothecary table. Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the theories you know and replace it with another theory that you could learn at that level.

classFeature.name: Experimental Research
entries: Choose one spell from the {@filter Apothecary, Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list|spells|level=!0;!6;!7;!8;!9|class=druid;wizard;cleric;apothecary (dk:ee'24)} that is no higher than your highest-level Apothecary spell slot. You always have that spell prepared and can cast that spell without expending a spell slot. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast this spell using an Apothecary spell slot. Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace your Experimental Research spell with another one of your choice.

classFeature.name: Apothecary Subclass
entries: You gain a Apothecary subclass of your choice. A subclass is a specialization that grants you features at certain Apothecary levels. For the rest of your career, you gain each of your subclass's features that are of your Apothecary level or lower.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify. You gain this feature again at Apothecary levels 8, 12, and 16.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your chosen discipline.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your chosen discipline.

classFeature.name: Greater Formula
entries: You have discovered esoteric theories, scraps of forbidden lore that you can use to augment your magical abilities. Choose one {@filter level 6 Apothecary spell|spells|source=|level=6|class=apothecary (Dk:EE'24)} as this formula.
You can cast your formula spell once without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again.

As shown in the Apothecary Features table, you gain another Apothecary spell of your choice that can be cast in this way when you reach Apothecary levels 13 (level 7 spell), 15 (level 8 spell), and 17 (level 9 spell). You also gain an additional spell of your choice for level 7 and 8 spells at Apothecary levels 19 and 20 respectively. You regain all uses of your Greater Formula when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Master Reformulation
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} and consult your formula book, you can replace one spell you gained from this feature with another spell of the same level from the Apothecary spell list.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Greater Formula
entries: You gain a {@filter level 7 Apothecary spell|spells|source=|level=7|class=apothecary (Dk:EE'24)} of your choice.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: At 14th level, you gain a feature granted by your chosen discipline.

classFeature.name: Greater Formula
entries: You gain a {@filter level 8 Apothecary spell|spells|source=|level=8|class=apothecary (Dk:EE'24)} of your choice.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: You gain the {@feat Ability Score Improvement|XPHB} feat or another {@5etools feat|feats.html} of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Greater Formula
entries: You gain a {@filter level 9 Apothecary spell|spells|source=|level=9|class=apothecary (Dk:EE'24)} of your choice.

classFeature.name: Subclass Feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your chosen discipline.

classFeature.name: Epic Boon
entries: You gain an {@filter Epic Boon feat|feats|category=EB} or another feat of your choice for which you qualify.

classFeature.name: Greater Formula
entries: You gain another {@filter level 6 Apothecary spell|spells|source=|level=6|class=apothecary (Dk:EE'24)} of your choice.

classFeature.name: Greater Formula
entries: You gain another {@filter level 7 Apothecary spell|spells|source=|level=7|class=apothecary (Dk:EE'24)} of your choice.

classFeature.name: Miraculous Recovery
entries: You can spend 5 minutes administering an occult formula into a number of creatures (which can include yourself) equal to your Intelligence modifier to facilitate an abnormally fast recovery. You must tend to the creatures during this period, using a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} with at least one use remaining. The targets regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, spell slots, {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, and other abilities as if they completed a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. A creature can only benefit from one Miraculous Recovery in a 24-hour period. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Mentalist
entries: {@i Reality is Perception, Manipulate Both}

The Amethyst Academy's influence stretches across Caspia, Westemar, and Elyria, but their power is often maintained through lies and misdirection. Mentalists are masters of deception, shadow politics, and information control. They weave enchantments that blur the line between truth and lies, crafting social manipulation so potent they warp memory and perception itself. In a world where truth is fragile, they are the unseen hands that shape the narrative.

subclassFeature.name: Manipulation Savant
entries: When you reach a Wizard level specified in the Manipulation Spells table, the spell is immediately added to your spellbook, and you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Manipulation Spells
rows: {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB}, {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB}, {@spell Suggestion|XPHB}

entries: Whenever you cast one of your Manipulation spells, you can do so without Verbal components.

subclassFeature.name: Behavioural Analysis
entries: You can add your Intelligence modifier to any Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}), Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}), or Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) check you make.
In addition, you know if a creature you can see has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Psychic damage or the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition, or if it has {@sense Truesight|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Mesmerizing Influence
entries: You gain the following traits.

entries.name: Point of Contact
entries: You can communicate with a creature charmed by you telepathically while it is within 1000 feet of you. Additionally, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can see through that creature's eyes and hear what it hears until the start of your next turn, gaining the benefits of any special senses it has.

entries.name: Remote Influence
entries: While you are using the senses of a creature charmed by you, you cast a wizard spell as if you were in that creature's space, instead of your own, using its senses. You can do so a number of times per day equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Mind Wipe
entries: When a creature charmed by you ends that condition, you can have that target make a Charisma save against your spell save DC. On a failure, the target does not remember they were charmed, and rationalize the situation to the best of their abilities.

subclassFeature.name: Feedback Loop
entries: You gain the following traits.

entries.name: Sympathetic Feedback
entries: When every target succeeds its first saving throw against a wizard spell you cast that would charm it on a failed saving throw, you regain a spell slot of a lower level than the spell you cast, up to a maximum of 5th level.

entries.name: Force Feedback
entries: When you cast a wizard spell that can cause the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition on a creature that has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition, you can choose to have the spell deal Force damage to the target instead of its normal effects. If that creature fails its saving throw against the spell, it takes Force damage equal to {@damage 1d10} per level of the spell slot used, or half as much damage on a successful saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Total Control
entries: When you cast a spell that would cause the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition on a creature, that creature doesn't gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on its saving throw against the spell if you or your allies are fighting it. In addition, when a creature is {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by one of your spells, it doesn't make a saving throw to end the spell when it takes damage (a spell that automatically ends if the target takes damage still ends as normal).

subclassFeature.name: Queen's Hand
entries: {@i Draw cards and let your hand decide your fate.}

Draw cards and let your hand decide your fate. The scoundrels and thieves of Buckledown Row are notorious gamblers, and everyone knows the best card sharps are stacking the deck and hiding an ace up their sleeve. Among the deadliest of them are those skilled enough to let the fate of the draw shape and warp their talents. A good gambler knows the right hand played at the right moment can change everything, and that a well-thrown card can cut deeper than any {@item Dagger|XPHB}.

Whether finging razor-edged cards in a back-alley brawl, bluffing their way through a high-stakes poker game beneath Buckledown Row, or dealing out fate with a fick of the wrist, the Queen's Hand Rogues are masters of the draw. With nothing more than a deck of cards in their pocket and a little gambler's luck on their side, they can pull off impossible heists, daring escapes, and deadly assassinations before their enemies ever realize the game has begun.

subclassFeature.name: Card Sharp
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item Playing Cards|XPHB} and the {@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB} skill. In addition, you can wield {@item Playing Cards|XPHB} as weapons. You treat them as Simple Melee weapons with the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light}, {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse}, and {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} (30/60) properties. On a hit, they deal {@damage 1d6} Slashing damage. {@item Playing cards|XPHB} have the {@itemMastery Nick|XPHB} weapon mastery property and doesn't count against the number of properties you can use with Weapon Mastery.

subclassFeature.name: Deal In
entries: As an action, you deal yourself a hand of cards. Roll a number of d6s equal to your Sneak Attack dice. Each die result determines a Card you gain, as shown on the Card Table below. These cards form your Hand.
Your Hand lasts until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, until all its cards are expended, or until you use this feature again.

You can play cards from your Hand to create special effects. Cards can be played individually, or two or more cards can be played together as a Combination if you have the cards required to create it. Once a card is played, either individually or as part of a combination, it is expended. Each card or combination specifies the conditions or action required to play it.

You can deal yourself a Hand twice. You regain one expended use when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You gain an additional use of this feature when you reach Rogue level 11.
If a card or combination effect requires a saving throw, the save DC equals 8 + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} + Dexterity modifier.

For example, A 9th level Rogue rolls {@dice 5d6} to determine their Hand. They roll 1, 3, 4, and 6, representing an Ace, two Nines, a Jack, and a King. Each card can be expended for its individual effect, but the two Nines could be expended together for the Pair combination effect instead of expending them individually.

entries.name: Individual Card Effects
entries: When you deal your Hand, the individual numbers on each die represent the Cards below. You can expend a single card from your Hand to create the corresponding effect.

items.name: Ace.
entries: When you use a {@classFeature Cunning Strike|Rogue|XPHB|5}, you can expend this card to pay for up to {@dice 2d6} of that {@classFeature Cunning Strike|Rogue|XPHB|5}. (No Action Required)

items.name: Nine.
entries: Once per turn when you hit with a weapon attack, you can expend this card to add {@dice 1d8 + 1} Force damage to the damage roll.

items.name: Ten.
entries: When you take a {@classFeature Cunning Action|Rogue|XPHB|2}, you can expend this card to regain {@dice 1d10} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

items.name: Jack.
entries: You can expend this card to reroll any ability check (no action required).

items.name: Queen.
entries: When you hit with a weapon attack, you can expend this card to add the {@itemMastery Push|XPHB}, {@itemMastery Sap|XPHB}, or {@itemMastery Slow|XPHB} weapon mastery to the attack.

items.name: King.
entries: When you fail a saving throw, you can expend this card to re-roll the saving throw with a bonus equal to half your Rogue level (rounded down).

entries.name: Card Combinations
entries: Your hand may contain matching or sequential combinations of Cards. You can play these combinations, expending all the cards accordingly.

items.name: Pair
items.entry: When you use your {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to take a {@classFeature Cunning Action|Rogue|XPHB|2}, you can expend two matching cards to take two {@classFeature Cunning Action|Rogue|XPHB|2|Cunning Actions}.

items.name: Three of a Kind
items.entry: When you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can expend three matching cards to treat all Sneak Attack damage dice rolled for that attack as though they rolled their maximum value (no action required).

items.name: Four of a Kind
items.entry: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend four matching cards to teleport up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space you can see and gain the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.

items.name: Full House
items.entry: On your turn, you can expend three matching cards and another two matching cards to immediately take one additional {@variantrule Action|XPHB}. You can use this {@variantrule Action|XPHB} only to take the {@action Attack|XPHB}, {@action Dash|XPHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, {@action Hide|XPHB}, or {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. If you attack using this action, you can apply your Sneak Attack even if you have already used Sneak Attack this turn.

items.name: Straight
items.entry: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend five sequential cards to attempt to incapacitate your target (no action required). The target must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed saving throw, the target becomes {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target can repeat the save, ending the condition on a success.

items.name: Five of a Kind
items.entry: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can expend five matching cards to roll five d20s and record the numbers rolled. Before a creature you can see makes a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can replace the roll with one of these recorded numbers. The creature must use the new roll. Once a recorded roll is used, it is expended. Any unused recorded rolls are lost when you use Deal In again or when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Card Tricks
entries: You have mastered some classic card tricks to make sure your cards always work in your favour. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Forced Draw
entries: Your attacks with playing cards deal Force damage instead of Slashing damage.

entries.name: Shuffle
entries: When you attack with playing cards you can attack twice instead of once when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

entries.name: Card Up Your Sleeve
entries: If you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no cards in your Hand, you can roll {@dice 1d6} and add the corresponding card to your Hand.

subclassFeature.name: Dead Man's Hand
entries: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can expend all remaining cards in your Hand. Roll {@dice 1d6} for each card expended in this way. Your current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} change to be equal to twice the total rolled, and you may immediately deal a new Hand. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Stack the Deck
entries: You have honed your card skills to the point where you can bend luck to your will. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Lucky Draw
entries: When you roll a natural 20 on an attack roll or ability check, you can immediately roll {@dice 1d6} and add the corresponding card to your Hand.

entries.name: Dealer's Choice
entries: When you deal yourself a Hand using Deal In, you can reroll one of the dice rolled. You must use the new result.

subclassFeature.name: Judgment Domain
entries: {@i Stand against the forces of darkness with the burning light of judgment.}

The Judgment Domain is claimed by clerics who walk unflinching into holy war. Those who do not merely defend the light but hunt the shadows that seek to consume it. These clerics are executioners of divine will, bearing radiant weapons and unyielding resolve. To them, no secret cult, lurking heresy, or power-hungry tyrant can hide forever; all crimes are revealed in time, and all must answer when judgment is called.

Many clerics of this domain march beneath the banners of the Silver Order, guided by the Sacred Flame into the heart of battle. Though the Flame is a force of redemption and acceptance, its light is not blind. Clerics of Judgment understand a hard truth—some will never seek redemption, and others will labor endlessly to extinguish the light itself. Against such foes, mercy is not denied, but judgment is inevitable.

The origins of the Judgment Domain lie in the wars against the Sorcerer Kings, when pacifists and keepers of peace were forced to take up arms against tyranny. In those dark years, the Sacred Flame burned not only as a symbol of hope, but as a sentence passed upon the corrupt. To see a cleric stride forward, radiant weapon in hand, was to know that the Flame had rendered its verdict, and that the guilty would not escape it.

subclassFeature.name: Soldier of Faith
entries: You are trained to deliver swift judgment to those who stand against your domain. You gain the following traits.

entries.name: Weapon Mastery
entries: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery properties of one kind of weapon of your choice with which you have proficiency, such as a {@item Longsword|XPHB}, or {@item Warhammer|XPHB}. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change the kind of weapon you chose. For example, you could switch to using the mastery properties of {@item Flail|XPHB|Flails}.

entries.name: Warrior's Wisdom
entries: You may select {@filter Fighting Style feats|feats|category=FS} as if you had the Fighting Style feature.

entries.name: Sense Fear
entries: You know if a creature within 120 feet has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

subclassFeature.name: Domain Spells
entries: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Judgment Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Judgment Domain Spells
rows: {@spell Command|XPHB}, {@spell Wrathful Smite|XPHB}, {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB}, {@spell Cloud of Daggers|XPHB}, {@spell True Strike|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Dreadful Judgment
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you present your {@item Holy Symbol|XPHB} and expend a use of your {@classFeature Channel Divinity|Cleric|XPHB|2} to call forth divine judgment on your foes. Each creature of your choice that can hear and see you within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed saving throw, the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for one minute or until it takes damage. While a creature is {@condition Frightened|XPHB} in this way, attacks against them have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn. Moreover, you can cast one of your Cleric cantrips that has a casting time of an action in place of one of those attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Jury's Verdict
entries: Creatures {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against your spells.

In addition, when you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against a creature with an attack using a weapon, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Executioner's Decree
entries: Your cleric spells and Channel Divinity powers ignore {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, except against creatures with Legendary Resistance or Epic Resistance.
Creatures {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by you have {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Radiant damage.

subclassFeature.name: College of Heralds
entries: In Caspia, every noble scion has songs sung of their greatness. At Lento College, ambitious bards dream of joining the courts of the great Caspian houses, performing on the stage of the Tower of Swords, or announcing the charge at the Rex Colosseum. However, only the most accomplished performers, those who can weave glory, grandeur, and spectacle into a single verse, earn such acclaim.

College of Heralds bards dedicate themselves to magnifying the reputation of their companions. Through clever verse and magical rhetoric, they elevate their traveling retinue to heroic stature, turning allies into legends and champions into icons. Their words inspire courage, sway public opinion, and shape how the world remembers every deed. Wherever a Herald goes, sagas follow, regardless of whether the deeds were ever truly done.

subclassFeature.name: Boastful Braggart
entries: You have practiced the art of bragging. When you speak of the triumphs of your allies, everyone listens.

You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}, {@skill Intimidation|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check that involves you invoking the prowess and capabilities of your allies.

subclassFeature.name: Inspire Greatness
entries: You have gained the skills to inspire your party with your words and songs, bolstering their capabilities and filling their egos for the trials ahead.

When you cast a spell using a spell slot of 1st level or higher that targets only one ally, you can also grant that ally a {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} die as part of the same action, without expending a use of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1}.

subclassFeature.name: That's Not How It Happened
entries: The magic of your words can rewrite the story as it is told, expunging all records of any possible defeat.

When an ally who has one of your {@classFeature Bardic Inspiration|Bard|XPHB|1} dice is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, they can expend the die to instead drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} and immediately roll it, regaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the result.

subclassFeature.name: Glorious Proclamation
entries: Your songs and stories have reached far and wide, being told and retold, and over time, embellished. Your champions are now legends known to all who would oppose them.

When you cast a spell of 1st level or higher using a spell slot that targets only one ally, you can choose an additional ally within range to also be affected by the spell. Both allies can benefit from your Inspire Greatness feature.

subclassFeature.name: Academy Mage
entries: {@i Summon an Academy Guardian to protect you...}

Most wizards trained by the Amethyst Academy pursue respectable careers as researchers, craftsmages, professors, curators of magical artifacts, or the proprietors of humble potion and scroll shops far from the monsters and battlefields of the continent. Yet not every mage remains within the safety of academy walls. Field agents must brave dangerous frontiers, investigate planar rifts, recover lost arcane knowledge, and secure the rare magical reagents that keep the Academy's research alive.

These elite magical operatives are entrusted with a bespoke Academy Guardian, a magical construct bound to its master and purpose-built to protect them in the field. Forged from living enchanted metal or shaped from bound elemental forces, each Guardian is engineered to endure the harshest environments, carry powerful spells, and interpose itself between its mage and mortal danger.

An Academy Mage trusts their Guardian to bear the brunt of every expedition—hauling equipment, weathering deadly hazards, and standing between their master and the countless threats lurking in the world's most perilous places.

entries.name: Designer's Note: Academy Membership
entries: The Academy Mage represents a character whose magical talent has been shaped by formal academic training, but it is not intended to represent every member of the Amethyst Academy. Characters of any subclass might be students, graduates, professors, agents, or members of the Academy, while a character with this subclass might have left the Academy, been expelled, or otherwise no longer be affiliated with the institution. Likewise, membership in the Amethyst Academy and possession of Academy Rings are separate considerations. Academy Rings are magic items whose availability is determined by the Game Master and the character's relationship to the Amethyst Academy. Taking this subclass does not grant Academy Rings, nor are Academy Rings required to take this subclass.

subclassFeature.name: Academy Spells
entries: When you reach a Wizard level specified in the Academy Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

entries.caption: Academy Spells
rows: {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Illusory Script|XPHB}, {@spell Invisibility|XPHB}, {@spell Locate Object|XPHB}
{@spell Rary's Telepathic Bond|XPHB|Telepathic Bond}, {@spell Teleportation Circle|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Academy Guardian
entries: You bind a guardian construct to your will to protect you. As part of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can perform a ritual using your {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} to summon an {@creature Academy Guardian|EldritchDrakkenheim24} (see the stat block below). You determine the guardian's appearance when you summon it. It might resemble an animated suit of armor, an elemental manifestation, or some other magical creation. Its appearance has no effect on its game statistics.
The guardian is friendly to you and your allies and obeys your commands. If you die, the guardian vanishes.

entries.name: The Guardian in Combat
entries: In combat, the guardian acts during your turn. It can move and take its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action unless you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to command it to take another action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, the guardian acts on its own and isn't limited to the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action.

entries.name: Restoring the Guardian
entries: If the guardian has been destroyed within the last hour, you can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action while holding your {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} and expend a spell slot. The guardian reappears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you after 1 minute with all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored.

subclassFeature.name: Protective Guardian
entries: When you take damage while your {@creature Academy Guardian|EldritchDrakkenheim24} is within 60 feet of you, your guardian can use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} toward you without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. If your guardian ends this movement within 10 feet of you, it takes the damage instead.

subclassFeature.name: Spell-Storing Guardian
entries: Your {@creature Academy Guardian|EldritchDrakkenheim24} can store spells cast into it, holding them until it produces their effects. The guardian can store up to 5 levels worth of spells at a time.

You can cast a Wizard spell of 1st through 5th level with a casting time of one action into the guardian by touching it as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect other than to be stored in the guardian. If the guardian can't hold the spell, the spell is expended without effect. The level of the spell slot used to cast the spell determines how much space it uses.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, the guardian can cast any spell stored within it. The spell uses your spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability modifier, but is otherwise treated as though the guardian cast the spell. The spell produced by the guardian is no longer stored within it, freeing up space. If a spell produced by the guardian requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you must maintain the spell's {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
Spells stored in the guardian remain until they are cast or the guardian is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

While the Guardian has five levels worth of spells stored in it, its Arcane Overcharge trait triggers on an attack roll of 19 or 20, and you can roll twice on the {@table Arcane Anomalies|MonstersOfDrakkenheim|Arcane Anomaly} table and choose which effect occurs.

subclassFeature.name: Self-Repairing Guardian
entries: While your {@creature Academy Guardian|EldritchDrakkenheim24} has at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} remaining, it regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Intelligence modifier at the start of each of your turns.

subclassFeature.name: Alienist
entries: An Alienist seeks to master the mysteries of the mind. Having devoted years to studying the inner workings of the brain, and unlocking its latent potential, an Alienist is able to read and manipulate the thoughts of others, and bend reality itself. They tap into unfathomable realities and shift the world to their will using the psychic energies they call upon from eldritch lore and arcane knowledge.

subclassFeature.name: Advanced Psychology
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with another skill of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Alienist Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Alienist Spells table. These spells count as Apothecary spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Apothecary spells you prepare.

entries.caption: Alienist Spells
rows: {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB}, {@spell Eldritch Blast|XPHB}, {@spell tasha's hideous laughter|XPHB|Hideous Laughter}, {@spell Suggestion|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Mental Influence
entries: You have studied hard to unlock the deepest recesses of your psyche, unleashing untapped psychic potential within yourself. This energy is represented by a pool of Psychic Points. You have four Psychic Points to start at 3rd level. You gain two more at 6th level, another two at 10th, and two more again at 14th level for a total of 10, which you can expend to use the various psionic powers you have detailed below. If you have expended all your Psychic Points, you cannot use these powers. You regain all your expended Psychic Points when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Cerebral Breakthrough
entries: When you cast an Apothecary spell that deals damage, you can expend a Psychic Point to re-roll any number of the dice. You must use the new rolls.

entries.name: Mind Over Matter
entries: Whenever you take damage, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend a Psychic Point, reducing the damage taken by an amount equal to {@dice 1d4} + your Apothecary level.

entries.name: Mind Whisperer
entries: When you cast an Apothecary spell which does not deal damage, you may spend a Psychic Point to cast it without any somatic or verbal components. If this spell affects another creature, they are unaware that the spell originated from you.

entries.name: Metaphysical Tether
entries: As an action, you can choose any number of creatures within 10 feet of you up to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and expend a Psychic Point. You immediately create a psychic link with each creature which lasts a number of hours equal to your Apothecary level or until you end the link (no action required). All the creatures tethered in this way can communicate telepathically with one another as long as they are within 100 feet of each other.

subclassFeature.name: Force Manipulation
entries: Your mental prowess allows you to control the very fabric of your own reality around you, allowing you to defy gravity and push yourself through the air using only your mind. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a Psychic Point to give yourself a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for 1 minute, during which you can {@variantrule Hover|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Mental Magic
entries: You gain the following additional psionic powers:

entries.name: Prescribed Effects
entries: Once per turn when a target you can see within 30 feet of you succeeds on an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw against a spell you cast, you can expend a Psychic Point to fill its mind with scrambled psionic energy, forcing it to reroll the {@dice d20} and use the lower roll for its saving throw, possibly turning a success into a failure.

entries.name: Mentally Prepared
entries: Once per turn, when a target you can see within 30 feet of you, including yourself, fails an Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma saving throw, you can use a Psychic Point and add your Intelligence modifier to the creature's saving throw, potentially turning the failure into a success.

entries.name: Fight or Flight Response
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend a Psychic Point and teleport up to 30 feet to an compounds in the field, and are always keen to unoccupied space you can see. You immediately gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Self-Diagnosis
entries: If you have no Psychic Points remaining, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend a spell slot to regain {@dice 1d4 + 1} Psychic Points. You also gain the following psionic powers:

entries.name: Careful Examination
entries: Whenever you cast a spell which restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature, you may expend a Psychic Point. If you do, all creatures targeted by the spell regain an additional amount of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Intelligence modifier.

entries.name: Enhanced Cerebellum
entries: When you cast any spell of 1st level or higher from your Alienist Spells feature, you can cast it by expending a spell slot as normal or by spending a number of Psychic Points equal to the spell's level.

subclassFeature.name: Impenetrable Mind
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to being {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB}. Magic cannot put you to sleep and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells that attempt to read your thoughts or control your mind or actions, such as {@spell Dominate Person|XPHB}. Additionally, when you use the Self-Diagnosis feature to regain Psychic Points using a spell slot, you now regain Psychic Points equal to the level of spell slot used.

subclassFeature.name: Chemical Compound
entries: You gain proficiency in {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Chemist
entries: A Chemist is a studied scientist whose advanced research in to chemical compounds has granted them the ability to terrorize their enemies with alchemical explosions, devastating gasses, and other horrific concoctions. Chemists love testing their new compounds in the field, and are always keen to advance their study and research further, finding new combinations to combust their foes with.

subclassFeature.name: Chemist Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Chemist Spells table. These spells count as Apothecary spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Apothecary spells you prepare.

entries.caption: Chemist Spells
rows: {@spell Melf's acid arrow|XPHB|Acid Arrow}, {@spell Burning Hands|XPHB}, {@spell Fire Bolt|XPHB}, {@spell Flaming Sphere|XPHB}, {@spell Ice Knife|XPHB}, {@spell Ray of Frost|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Precise Application
entries: When you cast a spell that deals Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison damage to other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to your Intelligence modifier. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

subclassFeature.name: Scientific Method
entries: Your dedicated research to chemical compounds and their direct applications and uses grant you additional abilities in the field.

entries.name: Chain Reaction
entries: When you cast an Apothecary cantrip that normally targets only one creature and deals Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison damage, the spell can instead target two creatures that are within the spell's range.

entries.name: Chemical Conditioning
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can give a creature you are touching (including yourself) {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison damage for 1 hour. This benefit ends immediately if you use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Bottled Spells
entries: You can store a spell from your Chemist Spells feature as a liquid in a small vial or bottle you touch when you cast the spell. The spell has no effect other than to be stored in the bottle. The spell remains stored inside until a creature opens the bottle. Once you use this feature, any unused bottled spell you previously created becomes inert, and you can't bottle another spell until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}. Any creature holding the bottle may spend an action to open it and cast the spell inside. The spell uses your spell attack bonus and save DC, but the spell treats the creature who released it as the caster in all other respects.
Starting at level 18th level, you can have two bottled spells active instead of one.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Adjustment
entries: When you cast a spell using an Apothecary spell slot which deals a type of damage from the following list, you can change that damage type to one of the other listed types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. Additionally, you can spend a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to analyse a creature you can see within 100 feet of you. You immediately know if it has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}, {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to any of the above listed damage types.

subclassFeature.name: Chemical Cataclysm
entries: When you cast a spell that deals Acid, Cold, Fire, Lighting, or Poison damage using an Apothecary spell slot, it is treated as if it was cast using a 7th-level spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Paranormal Practices
entries: You gain the ability to use your occult lore to influence paranormal entities around you, vanquishing their presence, or harnessing their powers.

When you use this feature, you choose which effect to create. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your intelligence modifier and regain all expended uses on a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Exorcism
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you use your paranormal science to strip away harmful influences. Choose one creature within 5 feet of you.

You end one of the following conditions affecting the target: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}. Alternatively, you can end one curse affecting the target. If the target is under the effect of a spell or ability that allows another creature to control its actions, you end that effect on the target. If a creature caused the condition, curse, or control effect you ended, that creature takes Psychic damage equal to {@dice 2d8} + your Apothecary level.

entries.name: Warding Rituals
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you call upon the energy of spirits or planar entities to ward you and your allies. Choose a number of creatures within 10 feet of you up to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You create a ward bound to those creatures, each creature is affected by the same ward. The ward lasts for 10 minutes, or until you choose to end it (no action required), use this feature again, or if you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. They gain one of the following benefits, chosen by you when you create the ward.

items.name: Ward of Fiend-Slaying
items.entry: Once per turn, the target can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll against a single target.

items.name: Ward of Celestial Shelter
items.entry: The target's {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} increases by an amount equal to half your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, rounded down.

items.name: Ward of Fae Unbinding
items.entry: The target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Psychic damage, and has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

items.name: Ward of Undead Salvation
items.entry: The target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic and Poison damage, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum and ability scores can't be reduced.

items.name: Ward of Aberrant Vigor
items.entry: The target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} and Constitution saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Exorcist
entries: An Exorcist comes prepared to deal with evil entities, sinister spirits, and fearsome fiends. Equipped with their holy symbol, a book of prayers, and a bag of ritualistic baubles, they stand ready to face the most unnatural and otherworldly entities of the realms. An Exorcist is accustomed to facing down insurmountable threats, with a holy symbol in hand they fear nothing, and stand as a shield between the realms of men, and those of monsters.

subclassFeature.name: Exorcist Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Exorcist Spells table. These spells count as Apothecary spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Apothecary spells you prepare.

entries.caption: Exorcist Spells
rows: {@spell Bless|XPHB}, {@spell Guidance|XPHB}, {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB}, {@spell Sacred Flame|XPHB}, {@spell Spiritual Weapon|XPHB}, {@spell Zone of Truth|XPHB}

subclassFeature.name: Spiritual Study
entries: Due to your scientific study of evil spirits, cClestials, Undead, Fey, and Fiends, you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Equipped For Field Study
entries: You gain proficiency in Heavy armor.

entries.name: The Science of Faith
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Religion|XPHB} and {@skill History|XPHB} skills. If you already have these proficiencies, you gain proficiency with another skill of your choice.

entries.name: Haunt Inspector
entries: You can sense Undead, Celestial, Fey, or Fiends within 120 feet of you and have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Perception|XPHB} and {@skill Investigation|XPHB} checks to spot or track them.

subclassFeature.name: Negation
entries: You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition, and cannot be possessed or cursed. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}.

As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a hostile creature you can see targets you or an ally within 60 feet of you with an attack, spell, or other effect, you can expend an Apothecary spell slot or a use of your Paranormal Practices feature to force it to make a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, the action they were attempting fails and resources used are wasted.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Ward
entries: Your ward anchors your allies' vitality. When a creature affected by your Warding Rituals is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it can make a Constitution saving throw with a DC equal to 5 + the damage taken. Instead of making this saving throw, the creature can choose to end the ward on itself, automatically succeeding on the save.

On a success, the creature drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead of 0, and regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Apothecary level. If the creature ended the ward to automatically succeed, it instead regains twice that number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Protecting Rites
entries: You can channel occult energy into your healing tinctures and spells. You gain the following features.

entries.name: Empowered Healing
entries: Whenever you cast a spell that targets a single creature and heals them, that spell deals maximum healing and the creature immediately gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Apothecary level.

entries.name: Spectral Link
entries: Whenever you use a spell or feature that heals an ally, you also gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Pentad Ward
entries: Instead of choosing a single effect for your wards, affected creatures gain the benefits of all your wards.

subclassFeature.name: Mutagenist
entries: Mutagenists have researched a serum that they inject to transform into a monstrous form. They use themselves as the conduit for this power and hurl themselves towards their enemies as a hulking abomination. They spend their lives researching and discovering new ways to improve and modify the serum in hopes of becoming the perfect form.

subclassFeature.name: Mutagenist Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Mutagenist Spells table. These spells count as Apothecary spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Apothecary spells you prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Natural Philosophy
entries: You gain proficiency with {@item Alchemist's Supplies|XPHB} and either the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB} or {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}. You also gain proficiency in two additional languages and the {@skill Nature|XPHB} skill. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with another skill of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Transmogrifying Elixir
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you may expend a spell slot to undergo a grotesque transformation. The transformation ends after one minute, or if you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}. When you undergo this transformation you maintain your personality, alignment, and game statistics. However, several changes occur:

items: Your Strength and Intelligence ability scores are swapped.

Your size becomes Large, your {@variantrule Jumping|XPHB|Jump} distance is doubled, and you count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

Each of your hands transform into a big meaty fist, which you can use to make melee weapon attacks. It counts as a simple weapon for you. Your big meaty fists deal {@damage 1d10} + your Strength modifier + your Apothecary level Bludgeoning damage on a hit.
You cannot cast or {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrate} on spells.
You have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to a range of 120 feet.
You gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to five times your Apothecary level.

entries: You also amplify your abilities based on the potency of your concoction. This is related to the spell slot you used to undergo the transformation:

items: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet per level of spell slot used.
Your AC is 13 + the level of spell slot used.

At the start of each of your turns and as long as you have at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} remaining, you regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the level of the spell slot used.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Biology
entries: When you are in your transmogrified form, you have more powerful attacks capable of bypassing your enemy's greatest defenses. Your big meaty fist attacks count as magical for overcoming {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to non-magical attacks and damage.

When you hit with an attack using your big meaty fists, you can expend a spell slot to empower the force of your blow with breaking unnatural energy. When you expend a spell slot in this way you deal an additional {@damage 1d8} Force damage per level of spell slot used.

subclassFeature.name: Adaptive Genetics
entries: You are able to use your changed biology to protect yourself from the clutches of death. When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in either your natural, or transmogrified state, your other self is ready to take over.

items: When in your natural form and reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you can immediately transform using your Transmogrifying Elixir feature expending a spell slot as normal. You regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Apothecary level when you do (in addition to gaining {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} from your transformation).

When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} in your transmogrified form, you revert to your natural form with 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} remaining. If you do, you can't assume your transmogrified form again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Unnatural Evolution
entries: You can use your Greater Formulas feature to transmogrify as if you had used a spell slot of that level. When you transform in this way you gain one of the additional genome mutations listed below.

items: {@i {@b Draconic Genome.}} You grow fleshy wings from your back and your skin takes on a scaly appearance. You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your walking {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} and as an action can spew bile in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. All creatures in the area must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@damage 8d6} Acid damage, or half as much on a successful save. Once you use this feature, you can not do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

{@i {@b Giant Genome.}} You become a hulking mass of flesh and muscle, turning yourself into a living siege weapon. Your size becomes Huge and all damage dealt to objects and structures is now doubled. As an action, you can expend a spell slot on your turn to slam your fists into the ground, sending shockwaves and rubble crashing around you. All creatures in a 30-foot radius {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or they take {@damage 8d6} Bludgeoning damage and are knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}. If they succeed on the saving throw, they take half damage and are not knocked {@condition Prone|XPHB}.

{@i {@b Cerebellum Genome.}} You maintain a fraction of your mind as you transform, allowing remnants of your studies to linger in your monstrous brain. You now have the ability to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on spells while in your transmogrified form. While transmogrified, your {@status Concentration|XPHB} can't be broken as a result of taking damage.

subclassFeature.name: The New Flesh
entries: You have perfected your Transmogrifying Elixir, breaking the confines of evolution and pushing your form to what you deem the apex of evolution. You gain the following benefits while transmogrified:

items: As long as you have one or more {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} remaining, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend a spell slot to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to ten times the level of the spell slot used.
If you fail a saving throw, as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} you can expend a spell slot to succeed instead.
Your Strength score becomes equal to your Intelligence score when transmogrified. Additionally, you can cast spells while in your transmogrified form.

subclassFeature.name: Mutant Strain
entries: You can evolve your diseases to cause new symptoms. You learn two mutant strains of your choice, and each time you gain a level in this class, you can replace one mutant strain you know with a different one from this feature. When you reach certain levels in this class, you learn additional mutant strains, as shown in the Mutant Strains Known table.

Whenever you cast a spell which inflicts diseases (such as {@spell Infect|CommunityDrakkenheim} or {@spell Contagion|XPHB}) you can apply an additional effect from your mutant strain to one of the targets of the spell, which lasts for the duration of that spell.
The following mutant strains are available to you when you learn a mutant strain. If a strain has a level requirement, you must be at least that level in this class to learn the strain.

items.name: Dizziness
entries: The target can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions}.

items.name: Fatigue
entries: Each time the target fails a saving throw against your disease, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to five feet until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Coughing Fits
entries: At the start of each of the target's turns, it must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it is unable to speak until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Shivers
entries: The target shakes uncontrollably. At the start of each of the target's turns, it drops whatever it is holding in its hands.

items.name: Weakness (9th level)
entries: The target deals half damage with melee weapon attacks.

items.name: Dry Eyes (9th level)
entries: Each time the target fails a saving throw against your disease, it becomes {@condition Blinded|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Intense Fever (9th level)
entries: The target can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.

items.name: Lesions (9th level)
entries: The target loses any damage {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistances}.

items.name: Nausea (15th level)
entries: Each time a target fails a saving throw against your disease, it becomes {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} until the start of its next turn.

items.name: Dystrophy (15th level)
entries: The target becomes {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB|Vulnerable} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

subclassFeature.name: Pathogenist
entries: The study of diseases opens doorways to many dangerous scientific discoveries. As experts in both ridding the body of sickness and the mutations of infectious disease, Pathogenists bring their study to the battlefield by using monsters and enemies as test subjects for their infections. Long years spent carefully monitoring the causes and effects of such plagues allows them to be experts at disease mutation, allowing their sickness to spread in a terrifying manner and riddle their foes with debilitating symptoms, crippling their bodies and rattling their minds.

subclassFeature.name: Pathogenist Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Pathogenist Spells table. These spells count as Apothecary spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Apothecary spells you prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Pernicious Pathogens
entries: Whenever a creature casts a spell or uses a trait or feature that would cure or remove one of your diseases (such as casting {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} to remove your {@spell Infect|CommunityDrakkenheim} spell), the creature must first make an ability check using its spellcasting ability (or its Charisma modifier, if the spell or special ability does not specify a spellcasting ability score). The DC equals your spell save DC.
On a failed check, the creature's spell, trait, or feature fails and has no effect. Any material components used are expended, and any spell slot expended is wasted.

subclassFeature.name: Plague Proficiency
entries: You learn the {@spell Chill Touch|XPHB} cantrip. You gain proficiency with the {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}. If you already have this proficiency, you gain proficiency with one other type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Breakthrough Infection
entries: When you cast a spell using an Apothecary spell slot or your Greater Formula feature that causes disease, that spell ignores any creature's {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to disease. Such creatures instead make any saving throws against your diseases with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Outbreak
entries: When a creature infected by one of your diseases dies, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to spread the disease to a different creature you can see within 30 feet of that creature.

subclassFeature.name: Insidious Incubation
entries: When you start casting {@spell Infect|CommunityDrakkenheim}, you can modify it so that it doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting. You must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before using this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Immunocompromised
entries: A creature infected with one of your diseases (such as {@spell Infect|CommunityDrakkenheim} or {@spell Contagion|XPHB}) has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws against your Apothecary spells.

subclassFeature.name: Rapid Evolution
entries: Whenever you cast a spell which inflicts diseases, you can apply two additional effects from your mutant strain to one of the targets of the spell, instead of one.

subclassFeature.name: Corpsewrought Creature
entries: You have constructed a companion from scavenged body parts and brought it to life with your occult magic: a {@creature corpsewrought creature|CommunityDrakkenheim}. The creature is friendly to you and your companions, and it obeys your commands. See its game statistics in the accompanying {@creature Corpsewrought Creature|CommunityDrakkenheim} stat block, which uses your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} (PB) in several places. You can determine the cosmetic characteristics of the creature; your choice has no effect on its game statistics.

In combat, the creature shares your {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, unless you take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn to command it to take another action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. If you are {@condition incapacitated}, the creature can take any action of its choice, not just {@action Dodge|XPHB}.

If your corpsewrought creature has died within the last hour, you can use your {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} as an action to revive it, provided you are within 5 feet of it and you expend an Apothecary spell slot. The corpsewrought companion returns to life after 1 minute with all its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored.

At the end of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create a new corpsewrought creature if you have your {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} with you and access to suitable "materials." If you already have a corpsewrought creature from this feature, the first one immediately perishes. The creature also perishes if you die.

subclassFeature.name: Reanimator
entries: A Reanimator is the master of life and death. Years spent perfecting their formula to bring the recently dead back, or to construct new life out of discarded flesh and patchwork anatomy, have left most Reanimators in a state constantly riding the lines between genius and madness. A Reanimator's skills at clutching life from the jaws of death make them exquisitely suited for dealing with death, and undeath. Their mad genius is presented with the construction of a creature made from the scrap body parts left behind by their enemies which storms forward into battle on their command.

subclassFeature.name: Reanimator Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared after you reach particular levels in this class, as shown in the Reanimator Spells table. These spells count as Apothecary spells for you, but they don't count against the number of Apothecary spells you prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Spark of Life
entries: You learn the {@spell Shocking Grasp|XPHB} and {@spell Spare the Dying|XPHB} cantrips. These count as Apothecary spells for you, and don't count against the number of Apothecary cantrips you know.

subclassFeature.name: Bodyguard
entries: When a creature within 5 feet of your corpsewrought creature makes an attack against you, it can use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make a melee weapon attack against the attacking creature. In addition, your corpsewrought creature's slam attack counts as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
In addition, you can cast {@spell Animate Dead|XPHB} once using an Apothecary spell slot. Once you do, you can't cast the spell again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Reanimated Head
entries: You have reanimated the head of a dead humanoid creature which now serves you in your studies. You may use the head as a spellcasting focus. The head speaks common and one other language of your choice.

Whenever you make an Intelligence ability check that lets you add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10. Additionally, you gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} and {@skill Investigation|XPHB} skills.

You can use the head to cast {@spell Speak with Dead|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. Once you use this feature you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Behold, My Creation
entries: Your corpsewrought creature's size increases to Large, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases to 40 feet.

When your corpsewrought creature hits a creature with a melee attack, it can immediately make a grapple attempt against the target. The DC to escape this grapple is equal to 8 + the corpsewrought creature's strength modifier (+4) + your PB.

subclassFeature.name: Berserk Fury
entries: When you command your corpsewrought creature to take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, the creature can make two attacks.

subclassFeature.name: I Can't Stop The Monster I Created
entries: Your corpsewrought creature increases in size to Huge, gains a reach of 10 feet, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases to 50 feet. Its slam attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage.
Additionally, the weight your corpsewrought creature can push or lift is doubled.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of the Seraphim
entries: When Saint Tarna was first called by the Sacred Flame, radiant beings are said to have descended from the heavens to mark her as the first paladin. Those who swear this oath follow in her footsteps, devoting themselves to the same celestial powers that touched her soul. They channel holy radiance, becoming living conduits of angelic might—divine messengers who burn with the light of justice.

Paladins of this oath are revered as holy champions and visionaries, guiding the faithful toward the Flame while delivering righteous judgment upon the wicked. They are prophets as much as warriors, speaking with the authority of the heavens and acting with the fury of angels.

entries.name: Tenets of the Oath of the Seraphim

items.name: Reveal the Truth.
items.entry: Burn away falsehood and shadow so that only truth remains.

items.name: Strike Down the Wicked.
items.entry: Strike at corruption wherever it festers; let no evil root endure.

items.name: Deliver Justice.
